domains.agency: Office of the District Attorney, Richmond County (Staten Island DA)
domains.platform: Self-hosted WordPress (Themeco 'Pro' theme) on SiteGround — off the NYC.gov platform, bot-walled by a SiteGround captcha
domains.tagline: A district attorney with no data, no API, and a marketing WordPress site the agents can't even read.
domains.netnew: TipSubmission — an API for the confidential drug/scam tip forms (today Contact Form 7 email only)
findings: The prompt's rcda.nyc.gov is dead — the Staten Island DA's real site is statenislandda.org, self-hosted WordPress (Themeco 'Pro' theme) on SiteGround, entirely OFF the NYC.gov platform.
ZERO NYC Open Data / Socrata datasets under any Richmond County / Staten Island DA agency label (verified). The office publishes no data at all.

The core prosecution data is DARK: cases, dispositions, and diversion outcomes live only in an internal case-management system and surface, if ever, as prose in a press release — no API, no dataset, no structured record.

The site is bot-walled: a SiteGround captcha (/.well-known/sgcaptcha/) blocks crawlers and agents from even reading the HTML, and the latent WordPress REST API (/wp-json) is unreachable behind it — so the content is not agent-accessible.
The one real inbound transaction is a pair of Contact Form 7 email forms (/drug-tip-form/, /scam-tip-form/) with no API and no trackable ticket — the net-new WRITE surface: TipSubmission.

All five NYC borough DA offices share the same functions (press, programs, victim services, tips, caseload stats) yet run on five separate stacks — a strong candidate for ONE shared five-borough DA API.

apis_observed.note: WordPress REST API exists (/wp-json/wp/v2/) but is unreachable behind the SiteGround captcha; undocumented and not intended as a public API. No OpenAPI.
The only inbound transaction — confidential drug/scam tips. Emails the office; no JSON contract, no ticket, no status read-back. The net-new WRITE surface.
SiteGround CDN captcha; 202s and redirects non-human requests, blocking robots.txt, the sitemap, and /wp-json. Makes the whole site agent-inaccessible.

apis_observed.public: yes, but empty for this agency
apis_observed.note: Verified ZERO datasets under any Richmond County / Staten Island DA label. The office has no Open Data presence.

domains.agency: New York City Tax Commission
domains.platform: NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' CMS (Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace + mPulse/Boomerang + AWS ALB) plus a browser-only online filing system; no distinct Tax Commission platform.
domains.tagline: An independent property-tax appeals body whose intake is PDF forms and whose only open data is a thin trail of outcomes.
domains.netnew: AssessmentAppeal — file a property-tax assessment appeal (Application for Correction).
findings: There is NO 'Tax Commission (TC)' agency in NYC Open Data; the Tax Commission's two datasets are published under 'Office of Administrative Tax Appeals (OATA)', the umbrella that now houses it.

The open data is thin and OUTCOME-ONLY: Assessment Actions (4nft-bihw, granted reductions/reclassifications) and Open Article 7 Petitions (aht6-vxai, judicial escalation to NY Supreme Court). Neither describes the appeal process itself.

The core transaction — filing an assessment appeal (Application for Correction) — has no machine-readable contract. It lives only in PDF forms (TC101/TC108/TC109/TC106/TC201…) and the Commission's online filing system, with a March 15/16 deadline and a $175 fee at $2M+ assessed value.
The domain sits on the shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace/mPulse/AWS ALB); the Tax Commission has no distinct platform of its own and no content or filing API.

Relationship: DOF SETS the tentative assessment (Notice of Property Value); the Tax Commission HEARS the appeal. Net-new write surface: file a property-tax assessment appeal (AssessmentAppeal). See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 2 datasets under 'Office of Administrative Tax Appeals (OATA)': Assessment Actions (4nft-bihw) and Open Article 7 Petitions (aht6-vxai). SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The only real, open API here — outcome data only.
Application for Correction (TC101/TC108/TC109/TC106 + income & expense TC201/203/208/214 + TC309). No JSON/OpenAPI surface; PDF forms and a browser-only filing flow.
About, how-to-appeal, forms, rules, guidelines, annual reports. No content API.
server-timing ak_p, alt-svc h3 on the informational site.
x-oneagent-js-injection; BOOMR mPulse snippet in page markup.
